steps.title: Set the two expressions equal
steps.content:
Since both $x + 47$ and $(x + 47)^2$ equal $y$, we can set them equal to each other:

$$x + 47 = (x + 47)^2$$

steps.title: Substitute to simplify
steps.content:
Let $u = x + 47$. This avoids expanding the right side and makes the equation much simpler:

$$u = u^2$$
$$u^2 - u = 0$$
$$u(u - 1) = 0$$

Therefore $u = 0$ or $u = 1$.

steps.title: Convert back and match to choices
steps.content:
Substitute back $u = x + 47$ to find the $x$ values:
  • When $u = 0$: $x + 47 = 0$, so $x = -47$
  • When $u = 1$: $x + 47 = 1$, so $x = -46$

Of our two solutions, $x = -46$ appears in the answer choices. That matches choice D — D is correct.
